entries.label: Five unsafe superpowers
entries.note: unsafe does NOT turn off the borrow checker; it only enables these five operations

entries.label: Unsafe block
entries.note: Wrap unsafe operations in an unsafe { } block; keep blocks as small as possible

entries.label: Unsafe function
entries.note: Marking a function unsafe means callers must uphold invariants not checked by compiler

entries.label: Safe abstraction over unsafe
entries.note: Best practice: expose a safe API that encapsulates unsafe internals

entries.label: Create raw pointers
entries.note: Creating raw pointers is safe; only dereferencing requires unsafe

entries.label: From references
entries.note: Cast references to raw pointers with as

entries.label: From address
entries.note: Creating from arbitrary address is safe; dereferencing it is UB unless valid

entries.label: Dereference
entries.note: Must be in unsafe block; pointer must be valid, aligned, and initialized

entries.label: Null and validity checks
entries.note: Always check for null before dereferencing; is_null() is safe to call

entries.label: Pointer arithmetic
entries.note: add/sub/offset for pointer arithmetic; must stay within allocation bounds

entries.label: Raw pointer vs reference
entries.note: Raw pointers opt out of Rust's safety guarantees; use only when necessary

entries.label: Defining unsafe function
entries.note: Document safety requirements in a # Safety doc section

entries.label: Calling unsafe functions
entries.note: Caller is responsible for upholding the documented safety requirements

entries.label: Unsafe methods
entries.note: Use unsafe methods for operations that can't be verified at compile time

entries.label: std unsafe functions
entries.note: Standard library provides unchecked variants for performance-critical code

entries.label: Static mut
entries.note: Accessing static mut is unsafe because of potential data races; prefer atomics

entries.label: Better alternative: atomics
entries.note: Atomics are safe and thread-safe; prefer over static mut

entries.label: Better alternative: OnceLock
entries.note: OnceLock for lazy one-time initialization; thread-safe without unsafe

entries.label: Defining unsafe trait
entries.note: Trait is unsafe when implementations must uphold invariants the compiler can't check

entries.label: Implementing unsafe trait
entries.note: unsafe impl asserts the implementor upholds the trait's safety contract

entries.label: Send and Sync
entries.note: Send and Sync are the most common unsafe traits; auto-derived for most types

entries.label: GlobalAlloc
entries.note: Custom allocator requires implementing unsafe GlobalAlloc trait

entries.label: Calling C functions
entries.note: Declare external C functions with extern "C"; calling them is always unsafe

entries.label: Exposing Rust to C
entries.note: #[no_mangle] preserves function name; extern "C" uses C calling convention

entries.label: C strings
entries.note: CString for owned null-terminated strings; CStr for borrowed; handle UTF-8 conversion

entries.label: Opaque types
entries.note: Use zero-sized private field for C opaque types you only access through pointers

entries.label: #[repr(C)] struct
entries.note: #[repr(C)] ensures struct layout matches C; required for FFI structs

entries.label: Callbacks
entries.note: Pass Rust functions to C as callbacks with extern "C" function pointers

entries.label: Define union
entries.note: All fields share memory; reading is unsafe because compiler can't know which is valid

entries.label: With ManuallyDrop
entries.note: Non-Copy types in unions need ManuallyDrop; you must manage cleanup manually

entries.label: Transmute
entries.code:
let x: u32 = 42;
let y: f32 = unsafe { std::mem::transmute(x) };
entries.note: Reinterpret bits as a different type; extremely dangerous; prefer safer alternatives

entries.label: Safer transmute alternatives
entries.note: from_bits, to_bits, from_ne_bytes, to_ne_bytes are safe alternatives

entries.label: Pin + unsafe for self-referential
entries.note: Self-referential types require Pin and unsafe; avoid if possible

entries.label: Inline assembly
entries.note: asm! macro for inline assembly; platform-specific and unsafe

entries.label: Unsafe guidelines
entries.note: Follow these practices to use unsafe responsibly
